subject: /home/budi/dev/prestashop-module/.claude/skills/psm-validate
verdict: POOR — the mandated compute_ready sweep found the class is not exhausted: static_layer hard-codes conclusive=true over versions where zero rules ever ran (CRITICAL, reproduced), and an empty version set makes ready=true over nothing under the strictest gate; separately the reuse gate reads ruleset provenance but ignores the tag-map provenance both container layers already emit.
summary: The ground rules from round 4 worked exactly as intended: every lens reproduced its findings by running the code, the determinism lens executed the mandated mechanical sweep of every compute_ready input instead of waiting for defects to surface by accident, and it found three surviving members of the class in one pass rather than five by coincidence across a whole round. The skill's primary strength is that its honest-degrade invariants hold everywhere they were actually built — all 10 psm_* config keys reach a real point of use, the false-pass invariants are intact by construction, and the resolver is genuinely the sole config mechanism. The primary opportunity is that `conclusive` is still asserted by construction in the two places nobody ever counted: Layer 1 declares itself conclusive unconditionally, and both gates that answer "is every target version proven" do it by iteration, so an empty set answers yes.

themes.title: Coverage asserted by construction, never counted
themes.root_cause: Two gates answer "was this actually evaluated?" without ever counting what ran. static_layer pins conclusive=true on the premise "Lapis 1 selalu jalan & selalu konklusif" — true of the script, false of the rules, which skip every version whose major is outside the ruleset domain. And compute_ready plus the unscanned-version gate both decide by iterating `versions`, so an empty set is vacuously proven. This is the same shape the code already recognized one nesting level down: the empty `--require ','` case is gated, with a source comment naming the mechanism exactly, while its twin one loop up is not. Both are reproduced, and the empty-versions seam has zero test coverage — mutating compute_ready to return a sentinel leaves the suite green.
themes.action: Make conclusivity a counted fact, not a constant, and gate the empty set at both seams. In ps-static-scan.py record per version `rules_evaluated` and echo `main_file_found`; in ps-aggregate.static_layer route `rules_evaluated == 0` and `main_file_found is False` into the existing `inconclusive_note` channel so ready drops while pass stays blind. Add the empty-target_versions gate in main() beside its already-gated twin, plus `if not versions: return False` inside compute_ready so the three sibling skills reading it are safe too — then add the mutation that is currently silent as a real assertion.

themes.title: Orphan signals: emitted with no reader, one seam over from where the class was last fixed
themes.root_cause: The recurring defect of this skill, still recurring. Three live instances: ps-flashlight-run emits `generated_config` specifically to mark an auto-neon phpstan run advisory, and nobody reads it — so an unenforced run is byte-identical to an enforced clean one, and its 7 carefully collected messages are dropped; both container layers emit per-version `tag`/`image`, and the freshness gate never reads them, so a bumped tag map reuses evidence from a different PrestaShop core and calls it fresh; `main_file_found` and `newest_source` are emitted with zero readers. The pattern is exact: the round that added ruleset provenance to the reuse gate reasoned, in its own docstring, that a layer's second input must be compared — then stopped at Layer 1 while Layers 2/4 have the same second input and already emit it. flashlight_layer even names this failure for the predecessor field ("Dulu dicatat di cs_note yang tak dibaca siapa pun = gap senyap"), and the advisory channel invented to solve it was never brought back to the layer that motivated it.
themes.action: Do one mechanical emitter-vs-reader inventory across all five scripts rather than fixing the three named sites. Give ps-plan-layers `--tag-map`/`--extra-tag-map` and compare recorded `image`/`tag` per version (generalize `plan_layer`'s `ruleset=` param into a per-layer provenance record, so Layer 3's second input has a home instead of being the next survivor). Reuse the existing advisory channel in flashlight_layer for `generated_config` and carry its messages through as warnings. Delete `main_file_found` and `newest_source`, or bind them.

themes.title: Input surfaces that solicit what nothing can consume
themes.root_cause: Three places ask for input that no consumer can use. On Activation step 3 promises to feed `tech/validator-rules.md` to `--extra-rules`, but that flag json.loads a path and the KB file is prose that explicitly defers to the skill's ruleset — the direction reversed and the prose still describes the old one, while also forbidding hand-application, so the branch fires every run and leaves no open path. output_folder is collected at setup and read by nothing in psm-validate. The anti-drift test guards only the 4 keys module.yaml declares, so the other 6 — and the docs table that is their only discovery surface — can drift freely, and its `len(shared) >= 4` floor is frozen at today's count so it can never notice the gap widening.
themes.action: Close each surface at its own end: drop the `--extra-rules` half of step 3 and keep the `--extra-tag-map` half that genuinely works (recovering the tokens the routing fixes need), or give KB rule augmentation a real seam — a JSON ruleset the KB actually ships. Make output_folder load-bearing by deriving the psm_reports_dir default from it, or document the independence as a decision. Iterate PSM_DEFAULTS rather than module.yaml in the anti-drift test, with an explicit allowlist for unprompted keys, so adding a key forces the author to choose a home.

themes.title: SKILL→reference boundary: a broken hop, an unrecognizable trap, and a gate fired too late
themes.root_cause: The always-loaded/routed split is mostly right, but four seams cross it badly. e2e-quickstart routes to `adversarial-lens.md` by a bare filename that does not resolve under the file's own stated convention — and that hop is itself a SKILL→reference→reference nest for a schema the reader is being asked to satisfy. The append-don't-overwrite trap on the adversarial layer file lives only in the routed reference, behind the words "Cara menulisnya", which read as format guidance: a model confident it can write JSON skips the load, overwrites Layer 3's findings, and the aggregate still returns conclusive:true over the wreckage. The port-leak gotcha is duplicated across the boundary and has already drifted — SKILL.md's copy omits the network cleanup the manual orchestrator needs. And ps-plan-layers' cheap spec validation is gated behind "resume an interrupted run", so on a fresh run a spec typo surfaces only after the full flashlight × versions × browsers boot.
themes.action: Fix the path (or cut the hop — the two rules the visual-defect writer needs are already restated inline). Move the one append-don't-overwrite clause into SKILL.md, since the model cannot recognize this trap. Make SKILL.md's port-leak command the complete one and drop the reference's duplicate. Widen ps-plan-layers' trigger from "interrupted run" to "before the expensive layers" — one clause of rewording; on a fresh run it marks all four, so nothing is lost.

themes.title: The token ceiling keeps forcing the trim/rebound cycle
themes.root_cause: SKILL.md is at 2995 against a hard budget of 3000 — headroom 5 — and this is the fourth consecutive round to arrive at or through the ceiling and trim in a hurry. The leanness lens verified the prose is NOT lying this round (a real improvement: past rounds found narration of logic the scripts owned, and one passage that flatly contradicted which component computed conclusivity), so the remaining bulk is largely load-bearing and the recoverable restatement is only ~95 tokens. That is not enough room for the fixes this round needs, which is why the routing theme has to be paid for out of the dead KB half of step 3 rather than out of further trimming.
themes.action: Take the two verified restatements (~95 tok): cut the four-layer enumeration from the Overview, which Validate defines authoritatively 15 lines later and the frontmatter already counts, and let the Vonis section own the pass-vs-ready teaching that Mode headless restates six lines on — keeping 'jujur, bukan cacat module', the one clause that guards a real caller mistake. Then take architecture-1's cut for real headroom. Do not trim further for tokens alone: past rounds proved trims here sever behavior and get reverted.

strengths: The round-4 ground rules demonstrably worked: every lens reproduced rather than read, and the mandated mechanical sweep found three members of the class in one pass — versus five found separately and by accident last round. Keep 'reproduce, do not read' and keep the fix author out of the judging seat.

Zero dead wires among config: all 10 psm_* keys were independently re-traced to a real point of use, including psm_flashlight_tag_map and psm_flashlight_ps_domain whose historical dead wires are confirmed closed. The resolver is genuinely the only config mechanism — no customize.toml, no second surface, no config.yaml parsing in the skill.

The false-pass invariants hold by construction: findings and errors stay mutually exclusive, per-browser failures land in browser_notes rather than the infra channel, and the deliberate degrade paths are honest. Four rounds of adversarial pressure have not broken them.

SKILL.md prose no longer misdescribes the scripts. The leanness lens checked the script-ownership claims that were false in earlier rounds — e2e_smoke_only dropping ready, advisory_note not gating, exit-2 semantics, --extra-rules adding vs --rules replacing, the no_php_at_root stop being genuinely the model's job — and all of them are accurate now.

All four deterministic pre-pass scanners are clean (0 findings), and all 5 scripts carry tests — the builder-side scanner fix (commit 30ff849) means that signal is now real rather than drowned in artifact false-positives.

recommendations.action: Close the two coverage gates that assert conclusivity without counting: rules_evaluated + main_file_found into static_layer's inconclusive_note, and the empty-target_versions gate in both main() and compute_ready. Add the currently-silent mutation as a real test — the seam has zero coverage today, which is why it survived five rounds.

Run one mechanical emitter-vs-reader inventory across all five scripts and bind or delete every orphan in a single pass. Highest-value binding is tag-map provenance in the reuse gate — it is a live honest-degrade hole that lets ready be true on evidence from a different core, and the SKILL.md cost is measured at -1 token.

Drop the dead --extra-rules half of On Activation step 3 (keeping the --extra-tag-map half that works), which both closes a promise the KB cannot deliver and frees the headroom the routing fixes need.

Fix the SKILL→reference boundary: resolve the broken adversarial-lens hop, inline the append-don't-overwrite clause, complete the port-leak command and de-duplicate it, and widen ps-plan-layers to run before the expensive layers rather than only on resume. Take the ~95 tokens of verified restatement first so these fit.

Make the config seam guarded rather than coincidental: bind output_folder or document its independence, and rewrite the anti-drift test to iterate PSM_DEFAULTS with an explicit allowlist so the 6 unprompted keys and the docs table stop being hand-maintained.

journeys.name: Interactive validation of a real module
journeys.steps: Resolve config → pick module + offer version/browser scope → Layer 1 static (stop if not a module) → Layer 2 flashlight in Docker → Layer 3 adversarial review via subagent → Layer 4 browser E2E + visual review by vision → aggregate into ready/pass. Sound end-to-end and proven against real containers across 1.7.8/8.1/9.1; the gaps this round are in what the verdict is allowed to claim, not in the flow.

journeys.name: Sibling skill gating on ready
journeys.steps: psm-develop / psm-scaffold / psm-cross-version read `ready`, not `pass`. This works, but determinism-1/2 mean a narrowed or empty scope can hand them a confident ready=true over versions nothing evaluated — the consumers cannot tell.

experience.headless: Strong and deliberately strict: returns a small JSON object (status complete|blocked, ready, pass, conclusivity, layers_run), never pulls images without a human, defaults --require to all four layers so a Docker-less runner honestly reports ready:false, and records assumptions to a memlog it inits first. The one soft spot is shared with the interactive path — an empty version set returns status complete with ready:true.

findings.title: static_layer claims conclusive:true for versions where zero rules were ever evaluated — ready certifies an unevaluated version
findings.evidence: REPRODUCED. A module with `<?php eval($x);` scanned via `--versions 8.1,1.6` gives 8.1 errors=1 pass=False, but 1.6 errors=0 pass=True — major '1' matches no rule's `affects` (domain is exactly 1.7|8|9), so all 26 rules skip and the version is scanned by nothing. Feeding that real artifact to `ps-aggregate.py --static st16.json --require static --versions 1.6` returns static {ran:true, conclusive:true, findings:[]}, pass:true, ready:true, exit 0. Same for `--versions 8.1,10.0`. static_layer is the only producer that can never emit inconclusive_note. Second producer at the same seam: when find_main_file returns None (ambiguous_main_file), every `files:["__MAIN__"]` rule silently no-ops — reproduced with an extra-rule present in both root files that never fires. main_file_found/reason/candidates are read by nothing in ps-aggregate: the named orphan class, still orphaned, masked today only by struct-compliancy happening to fire — a mask that vanishes for exactly the out-of-domain case above.
findings.recommendation: conclusive for Layer 1 is a countable fact, not a constant. Record per version `rules_evaluated` and echo `main_file_found` in ps-static-scan; in static_layer route rules_evaluated==0 and main_file_found false into inconclusive_note so ready drops while pass stays blind. Fix the class: validate_extra_rules already gates `affects` against MAJOR_KEYS and gates `affects:[]` as never-fires — the identical gate is missing on the TARGET side one seam over.

findings.title: Empty target_versions → compute_ready iterates nothing → ready:true over ZERO versions under the strictest gate
findings.evidence: REPRODUCED. A static artifact with `versions:{}` through `ps-aggregate.py --static st-empty.json` (no --versions, so the strict four-layer default applies) returns target_versions:[], pass:true, ready:true, exit 0 — on a box with no Docker — and positively asserts flashlight_conclusive:true and e2e_conclusive:true for layers that layers_run says did not run, two fields contradicting each other in one payload. This is the empty-container class the code already recognizes: `--require ','` filters to [] and IS gated, its source comment naming the mechanism exactly ("for layer in [] tak pernah jalan -> ready=true atas NOL lapis terbukti"); the outer `for m in versions.values()` is the same statement one level up, ungated. The unscanned gate cannot catch it: `[v for v in [] ...]` is []. TEST IS VACUOUS: mutating compute_ready to `if not versions: return "MUTANT"` leaves the suite fully green.
findings.recommendation: Add the sibling gate in main() beside the existing empty---require and unscanned-version gates: no target_versions → stderr + exit 2. Belt-and-braces `if not versions: return False` inside compute_ready so the three sibling skills reading it are safe, not just the CLI. Then add the mutation above as a real assertion — the --require twin has a test, this one has none, which is why the class survived.

findings.title: Auto-neon phpstan is an orphan signal: generated_config unread, findings discarded, advisory run byte-identical to an enforced clean run
findings.evidence: REPRODUCED. Two flashlight artifacts differing only in the coding_standard block — (a) auto-neon advisory with generated_config:true, warnings:7, 7 error_messages; (b) module-owned neon genuinely clean — produce byte-identical layer output through the aggregate: {state:pass, conclusive:true, errors:0, findings:[]}, ready:true both. `grep generated_config ps-aggregate.py` → no hits: the flag ps-flashlight-run computes specifically to mark the verdict advisory is read by nobody, and the 7 collected messages are dropped (flashlight_layer only reads cs['error_messages'] inside `if cs['errors'] > 0`, and the advisory branch pins errors:0 by construction). Correctly classified this is an OBSERVATION, not a coverage gap, so it must not drop ready — but the round-4 console-error precedent settled that observations must still be surfaced. flashlight_layer:170 even names this failure for the predecessor field: "Dulu dicatat di cs_note yang tak dibaca siapa pun = gap senyap."
findings.recommendation: Reuse the channel the class already has rather than inventing a fifth: add `advisory_note` to flashlight_layer for the generated_config case (naming the escape hatch — ship a module phpstan.neon) and carry error_messages through as severity:warning findings. That makes flashlight symmetric with e2e_layer's console-error handling and makes "phpstan hijau" distinguishable from "phpstan tak ditegakkan" for the siblings reading the payload. Mirror the existing advisory_note test — today test 7b only asserts the advisory does not block, exactly what a green test proves while the signal stays invisible.

findings.title: Reuse gate never reads the tag-map provenance both container layers already emit — stale evidence from a different core reused as fresh
findings.evidence: REPRODUCED. `plan_layer(..., ruleset=ruleset if layer == 'static' else None)` — its docstring names the class exactly ("Vonis static punya dua input — module dan ruleset — jadi membandingkan mtime module saja membuat aturan KB yang baru ditambahkan tak pernah menyala"), and that reasoning is verbatim true of Layers 2/4, whose second input is the tag map. The gate never learned it: grep for tag_map in ps-plan-layers = 0, and --help offers only --rules/--extra-rules. Both emitters already record provenance per version ({version, tag, image}); read by zero. A flashlight file recording image 9.1.4-nginx, planned against a bumped map, returns flashlight {reuse:true, reason:'lebih baru dari module & cakupan versi cocok'} — affirmatively certifying coverage it did not test on the target core. Not hypothetical: memlog records a real past bump (9.0→9.1, nightly→9.1.4-nginx, 8.1→8.1.6-nginx). Since SKILL.md:25 says run only layers marked rerun, Layers 2/4 are silently skipped and ready can be TRUE on evidence from a different core.
findings.recommendation: Port the provenance compare to the seam it was never carried to, in the script (zero SKILL.md cost): give ps-plan-layers --tag-map/--extra-tag-map, resolve the expected tag via fl.parse_tag_map through the existing load_sibling (keeping the single-owner rule), and return reuse:false with 'image berbeda dari yang memproduksi file lapis' / 'file lapis tak mencatat image yang memproduksinya', mirroring the three ruleset branches. Generalize plan_layer's ruleset= param into a per-layer provenance record so Layer 3's second input has a home rather than being the next survivor. SKILL.md:25 changes --extra-rules → --extra-* : measured -1 token.

findings.title: Two residual orphan keys emitted with zero readers: main_file_found and newest_source
findings.evidence: Mechanical emitter-vs-reader sweep, confirmed by running the scripts. main_file_found appears in a real static run's top-level output and greps to ZERO hits across ps-aggregate.py, ps-plan-layers.py, SKILL.md and references/ — named as a confirmed orphan in a prior round, still live. It is redundant with the signal that did get wired: main_file_reason is emitted exactly when the main file is unresolved, and both branches reproduce (no_php_at_root with candidates:[], ambiguous_main_file with candidates:['alpha.php','beta.php']), both genuinely read by SKILL.md:27. newest_source has exactly one mention in the whole skill — its own emit site — and is redundant with the reason string that already embeds the path. Reported low: neither has a behavioural consequence today; the value is that each is a live channel a future reader could bind to believing it is maintained.
findings.recommendation: Drop both, or bind them. Deleting main_file_found costs nothing — every consumer routes on main_file_reason/main_file_candidates. Deleting newest_source costs nothing — the path is already in the reason string operators read. No SKILL.md change either way. Do it in the same pass as enhancement-1 so the inventory is clean when the provenance seam is generalized, otherwise the next round re-derives the same sweep.

findings.title: On Activation step 3 promises a Layer-1 rule augment the knowledge base cannot deliver
findings.evidence: Step 3 says to read tech/validator-rules.md and pass additional scan rules (JSON schema'd like assets/ps-rules.json) as --extra-rules, adding "Jangan terapkan aturan dengan tangan". But ps-static-scan.py:331-337 json.loads --extra-rules as a path, and the KB file is prose with zero JSON fences that explicitly defers to the skill's ruleset ("Ruleset lengkap (regex per rule) ada di assets/ps-rules.json — rujuk ke sana untuk deteksi") — matching psm-agent-expert's commission to summarize, and this skill's own memlog:8 ("skill jadi sumber aturan, knowledge base nanti merujuk ke sini"). No JSON file exists to pass, SKILL.md never says to author one, and hand-application is forbidden — so no path is left open. The branch fires every run because the file exists. The --extra-tag-map half works: tech/flashlight.md:11 supplies the comma string verbatim. The two halves are stated symmetrically ("keduanya MENAMBAH") but only one is executable.
findings.recommendation: The direction reversed and SKILL.md still describes the old one. Drop the validator-rules/--extra-rules half and keep step 3 to what works (tech/flashlight.md → --extra-tag-map). If KB rule augmentation is still wanted it needs a real seam — a JSON ruleset the KB actually ships — and step 3 should name that path, not a markdown summary. This also recovers the tokens architecture-4 needs.

findings.title: e2e-quickstart routes to a second reference by a path that does not resolve under its own stated convention
findings.evidence: Line 8 declares all paths relative to the skill folder. Line 157 then routes to `adversarial-lens.md`, which under that convention resolves to psm-validate/adversarial-lens.md — nonexistent; the file is references/adversarial-lens.md, as SKILL.md:31 correctly names it. Path lint passed because a bare filename trips no rule. This is also the SKILL→reference→reference nesting the carve-by-relevance rule forbids: the visual-defect branch enters e2e-quickstart from SKILL.md:33 and is bounced to a third file for the schema it is being asked to satisfy.
findings.recommendation: Fix the path to references/adversarial-lens.md. Better, cut the hop: the two rules the visual-defect writer needs (severity:error blocks; top-level versions must cover the version seen) are already restated at e2e-quickstart.md:154-156, so replace the route with a plain statement that the aggregate rejects schema violations with exit 2, leaving adversarial-lens.md as the reviewer-subagent contract it is.

findings.title: The append-don't-overwrite trap on the adversarial layer file is under-signaled at the only point it fires
findings.evidence: SKILL.md tells the model to write blocking visual defects to the adversarial layer file — "satu-satunya kanal judgment model ke vonis" — then routes with "Cara menulisnya … references/e2e-quickstart.md". The trap lives only in that reference (:153-154): append to the existing findings, never overwrite, because Layer 3's reviewer findings are in that file. "Cara menulisnya" reads as format guidance, and a model confident it can write JSON will skip the load and use Write, which overwrites. The consequence is silent and total: adversarial_layer reads the clobbered file, finds only the visual finding, and still returns conclusive:true (ps-aggregate.py:377) — the verdict claims Layer-3 coverage whose findings were destroyed. Per the gotcha rule, a rule whose trap the model cannot recognize does not carve, and "writing a JSON file" is precisely an operation a model does not know is dangerous.
findings.recommendation: Move the one clause inline: SKILL.md:33 becomes "tambahkan ke findings yang ada di file lapis adversarial (jangan timpa — file itu memuat temuan Lapis 3)", leaving the rest of the mechanics routed.

findings.title: SKILL.md sits 5 tokens under budget with two gotchas duplicated across the boundary, one already drifted
findings.evidence: SKILL.md is 2995 against a hard budget of 3000 — warn tier (desired 2000), no room for the fixes above. The clearest trim is content duplicated in the reference, already drifted into two sources of truth: (a) port-leak cleanup — SKILL.md:29 gives the container command; e2e-quickstart.md:164 gives that PLUS the network removal. The manual orchestrator really does create a network (ps-flashlight-run.py:316-318, psm-fl-net-<uid>) and removes it only on clean teardown, so SKILL.md's copy is the incomplete one and leaks networks on every force-killed run. (b) the Playwright prerequisite at SKILL.md:33 restates e2e-quickstart.md:21 while routing to that same file in the same sentence.
findings.recommendation: Keep the port-leak gotcha inline — its trigger (next run fails to bind) is unrecognizable — but make it the complete command so the copies stop disagreeing, and drop the reference's duplicate. Cut SKILL.md's playwright install command down to the route it already carries, since a model needing the command is loading that file anyway. With architecture-1's cut this restores enough headroom for architecture-3.

findings.title: ps-plan-layers is routed as resume-only, so its cheap pre-boot spec validation never fires on a fresh run
findings.evidence: SKILL.md gates the script behind one branch: "Untuk melanjutkan run yang terputus…". But the script does two jobs, and its own docstring motivates the second as a fresh-run concern: authored E2E spec validation is cheap, but used to run only inside ps-e2e-run — after flashlight boot × N versions × M browsers — so one typo surfaced only after the expensive run finished; here the author can fix it first (:187-192, emitting e2e_scenario_notes). e2e-quickstart.md:178 states this as fact, but that file is not loaded by default, so the claim is asserted where no run reads it. On a fresh run the model skips plan-layers and a typo'd spec surfaces only after the full boot. The layer stays honest, just paid for late.
findings.recommendation: Widen the trigger from 'interrupted run' to 'before the expensive layers': run ps-plan-layers first on every run, use its rerun list to decide which layers execute (on a fresh run it marks all four, so nothing is lost), and fix any spec it flags before Lapis 2 boots. One clause of rewording, not new content.

findings.title: output_folder is collected at setup, then silently ignored by every psm report path
findings.evidence: Reproduced: the resolver emits communication_language and user_name but no output_folder (_CORE_KEYS covers only those two). Meanwhile merge-config.py:108 lists output_folder among the core keys psm-setup collects, and config.yaml:2 holds it. But psm_reports_dir bakes the same literal independently in three places (PSM_DEFAULTS, module.yaml:36, config.yaml psm section), and grep across psm-validate's SKILL.md, references and all five scripts returns zero readers of output_folder. An operator who answers output_folder with anything but the default still gets reports under _bmad-output/psm-validate, and nothing warns them. Same dead-wire shape as psm_flashlight_tag_map and user_name: a collected value dropped before its point of use, invisible because the duplicated literal happens to equal the default.
findings.recommendation: Pick one and make it load-bearing. Either add output_folder to _CORE_KEYS and derive psm_reports_dir's default from it (<output_folder>/psm-validate, with an explicit psm_reports_dir as override), or, if independence is intended, say so in the PSM_DEFAULTS comment and the docs/psm/README.md row so the duplication is a decision rather than a coincidence. Do not leave the two literals equal and unguarded.

findings.title: Anti-drift test guards value drift on 4 keys but cannot see membership drift on the other 6
findings.evidence: The test exists and passes (4 drift checks green) and reads live PSM_DEFAULTS via importlib. But its semantics are intersection-only: it walks module.yaml, not PSM_DEFAULTS, so a key in PSM_DEFAULTS and absent from module.yaml is never compared. That is the current state: PSM_DEFAULTS holds 10 psm keys, module.yaml declares 4, and installed config.yaml has exactly those 4. The other 6 (orchestrator, db_image, ps_domain, startup_timeout, e2e_enabled, e2e_browsers) reach real flags in psm-validate but psm-setup never writes them, so their only discoverable home is the hand-maintained table at docs/psm/README.md:46-55, which no test guards. The `len(shared) >= 4` floor is frozen at today's count, so it can never notice the gap widening: the next key added lands in neither module.yaml nor the docs table and the suite stays green.
findings.recommendation: Iterate PSM_DEFAULTS, not module.yaml, and assert coverage explicitly. Keep the four prompted keys as the asked set, but assert every PSM_DEFAULTS key is either declared in module.yaml with a matching default or listed in an explicit _UNPROMPTED_KEYS allowlist — so adding a key forces a choice of home. Then extend the test to parse the docs table and assert one row per key with a matching default, converting the only discovery surface for the 6 unprompted keys from a manual copy into a guarded one.

findings.title: Overview enumerates the four layers that Validate defines authoritatively 15 lines later
findings.evidence: The Overview holds stance, outcome, consumer and bar exactly as the canon prescribes — but mid-sentence it also enumerates all four layers and their kinds. Each kind is restated in its own Lapis heading in Validate, where the run conditions (selalu / bila Docker ada / bila psm_e2e_enabled ≠ false) also live. The ordering rationale the enumeration might be thought to carry is separately stated in the Validate lead. The four layers are named a third time in the frontmatter description. This is the canon's 'facts restated across sections' — and unlike the SKILL/e2e-quickstart overlaps, no carve rule justifies it: both copies sit in the same always-loaded file, read in one pass.
findings.recommendation: Cut the layer enumeration from the Overview and let the Lapis headings own it. Keep stance, outcome, consumer and bar untouched — those are the highest-leverage lines in the file and none depend on the enumeration.
findings.proposed_smallest: Bertindak sebagai validator module PrestaShop yang teliti dan jujur: operator memegang module, skill memegang aturan kompatibilitas lintas versi dan prosedur ujinya. Hasilkan **vonis berbasis bukti** apakah sebuah module sehat di PrestaShop **1.7.x, 8.x, dan 9.x sekaligus**. Konsumen hasil: operator (perbaiki sebelum rilis) dan workflow lain (psm-cross-version, psm-develop, psm-scaffold) yang memanggil skill ini sebagai gerbang mutu — jadi output harus JSON terstruktur yang dapat dibaca mesin, dengan ringkasan yang bisa ditindaklanjuti manusia.
findings.predicted_delta: Nothing, and ~55 tokens reclaimed. The enumeration is pure framing: every fact in it (each layer's kind, its run condition, why cheap-and-deterministic runs first) is stated with more precision in Validate, in the same always-loaded file. The only thing lost is knowing 'there are four' before reaching Validate — which the frontmatter already says. Route to variant eval; the run to watch is one where Docker is absent, since that is where layer-kind framing would matter most if it mattered at all.

findings.title: The pass-vs-ready distinction and the --require narrowing advice are each stated twice, six lines apart
findings.location: SKILL.md:39 (Vonis dan output) and SKILL.md:45 (Mode headless)
findings.evidence: Line 39 ends with the --require narrowing advice and "pass buta konklusivitas — ready yang menjawab siap-rilis". Line 45 says both again ("Gerbang CI membaca ready… pass tetap menampung temuan nyata. Menggating lebih sempit: nyatakan di --require"). A third copy lives in ps-aggregate.py's --require argparse help, nearly verbatim, which line 37 directs the model to read. The audience split (interactive summary vs headless JSON) does not justify the repeat: both sections are in one always-loaded file consumed in a single pass, so by line 45 the model has already read the distinction.
findings.recommendation: In Mode headless keep only what that section uniquely owns — the return contract (which fields, blocked+reason) and the strict default — and let line 39 own the pass-vs-ready teaching. If only one clause survives, keep 'jujur, bukan cacat module': it guards a real failure (a caller reading ready:false as a module defect) rather than restating a fact.
findings.proposed_smallest: Gerbang CI membaca **`ready`**, bukan `pass`: default `--require` keempat lapis, jadi runner tanpa Docker/browser menghasilkan `ready: false` — jujur, bukan cacat module.
findings.predicted_delta: Nothing, and ~40 tokens reclaimed. The dropped clauses are the second statement of facts already carried by line 39 in the same context window, plus a third statement of the argparse help. Confidence is lower than leanness-1 — the retained 'jujur, bukan cacat module' is deliberately preserved because readiness-gate semantics have been churned repeatedly and a past round already restored a severed line here. Route to variant eval on a Docker-absent headless run, exactly the case the cut clauses describe.
